steps.title: Multiply both sides to clear the fraction
steps.content:
Starting from $u - 3 = \frac{6}{t-2}$, multiply both sides by $(t-2)$:
$$(u-3)(t-2) = 6$$

steps.title: Solve for t
steps.content:
Divide both sides by $(u-3)$:
$$t - 2 = \frac{6}{u-3}$$
Add 2 to both sides:
$$t = 2 + \frac{6}{u-3} = \frac{2(u-3) + 6}{u-3} = \frac{2u - 6 + 6}{u-3} = \frac{2u}{u-3}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
